channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, Canada, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, Canada, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Call to Artists: Walden Fire Station and Library (Calgary Arts Development)
item.description:
Organisation: Calgary Arts Development (CADA)

Location: Walden community, Calgary, Alberta, Canada. Open to local and national (Canadian) professional artists/artist teams.

Award: Total project budget of CAD 275,000 (exclusive of all applicable taxes) for one selected artist/artist team. The artist fee for the total project is CAD 68,750 + GST. Budget breakdown can be adapted to the artist's approach, following CARFAC's recommendation that a minimum 20% of the budget go to the artist's fee; the total allocated budget will not be increased.

Duration: Concept and design from September 2026 to end of July 2027; fabrication and installation (Phase 2) from the second half of 2027 into 2028.

Deadline: 28 Jul 2026

Calgary Arts Development (CADA) is seeking an artist or artist team to join The City of Calgary's Walden Fire Station and Library project team to create permanent public artwork(s) for a combined fire station and library planned for the community of Walden. One artist/artist team will be selected with a total project budget of CAD 275,000 (exclusive of all applicable taxes); the opportunity is open to local and national professional artists/artist teams working in various media. ELIGIBILITY: local and national mid-career to late-career professional public artists and artist teams with a minimum of five years' previous experience creating artwork in the public realm; they must have at least three outdoor permanent public art installations, each with a lifespan of over 10 years and a minimum project budget of CAD 150,000 (see the Canada Council for the Arts definition of a professional artist). Walden does not currently have an active community association, so the project requires experience in artist-led community engagement and a strong understanding of alternative outreach. Artists must be available to meet the project timelines from September 2026 to the end of concept and design by July 2027; fabrication and installation take place in Phase 2, starting in the second half of 2027 and continuing into 2028. REWARD: total project budget CAD 275,000 (artist fee CAD 68,750 + GST); CADA is open to adapting the budget breakdown to the artist's approach, following CARFAC's recommendation of a minimum 20% to the artist's fee, though the total budget will not be increased. TIMELINE: applications open 16 June 2026 (9am MT); pre-recorded information session posted early July; application deadline 28 July 2026 (no applications accepted after 1pm MT); shortlisted interviews mid-September; results late September 2026. Eligibility questions: walden@calgaryartsdevelopment.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SAT x LAB7: Performing Arts and Technology Research-Creation Residency 2026 (Montreal)
item.description:
Organisation: Societe des arts technologiques (SAT) in partnership with LAB7 / 7 Fingers (Les 7 Doigts)

Location: Montreal, Canada. Open internationally; no nationality restriction stated.

Award: CAD 10,000 grant per selected project, plus roughly two weeks (about 10 days) of studio workspace at the 7 Fingers Creation Centre and personalised coaching from the SAT and LAB7 teams. No application fee.

Duration: Around two weeks (10 days) of in-studio residency.

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

The Societe des arts technologiques (SAT) and LAB7 (the research-creation lab of 7 Fingers / Les 7 Doigts) offer research-creation residencies at the intersection of performing arts and technology, on the theme of human movement. Each selected project receives a CAD 10,000 grant, roughly two weeks (about 10 days) of studio workspace at the 7 Fingers Creation Centre in Montreal, and personalised coaching from the SAT and LAB7 teams. Open to non-profit organisations, collectives and independent artists working between performing arts and technological arts; the call is effectively open internationally, with no nationality restriction stated. No application fee. Results are announced in early October 2026. Application deadline: 31 August 2026, 17:00. Apply via the official open-call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: City of Ottawa: Vanier Artist-in-Residence 2027 (RFQ)
item.description:
Organisation: City of Ottawa Public Art Program

Location: Vanier, Ottawa, Ontario, Canada (in-person residency)

Award: CAD 40,000 plus HST if applicable for the Artist-in-Residence (inclusive of all residency costs), with an additional CAD 40,000 to 70,000 available afterward to create a final legacy public artwork. No application fee.

Duration: Residency March 2027 to September 2027; legacy public art installation Summer 2028

Deadline: Closed 26 Jun 2026

The City of Ottawa Public Art Program invites professional artists or artist teams to submit qualifications to be Vanier's Artist-in-Residence, a community-led initiative designed to foster meaningful engagement and dialogue with the Vanier community, with the insights gained informing the creation of a final legacy artwork. The community is looking for a reliable, collaborative, and adaptable artist or team; the role will benefit from candidates with diverse community engagement experience, current or past ties to Vanier, and the ability to communicate fully in both official languages. After the residency, the artist or team will be asked to provide a proposal for a legacy artwork, with an additional budget and timeline to plan and implement it. This two-stage competition is held under the Public Art Policy as a Request for Qualifications. BUDGET: CAD 40,000 plus HST for the Artist-in-Residence (March to September 2027), inclusive of all residency costs such as consultations, research, program activities, materials, deliverables, and the artist's time, travel, and meeting attendance; plus CAD 40,000 to 70,000 for the legacy public art installation (Summer 2028), covering design, fabrication, insurance, storage, transport, installation, engineering, permits, and anchoring, as well as the artist's time and travel. ELIGIBILITY: an equal-opportunity project open to local, national, and international professional artists and artist teams with experience creating permanent public art and working with multidisciplinary teams; City of Ottawa employees are not eligible. Applications from First Nations, Inuit, and Metis artists are welcomed and encouraged. VISION: the selected artist participates in existing Vanier programming and establishes new opportunities for community interaction and collaboration, conducts historical research into the neighbourhood, and proposes a permanent legacy artwork celebrating Vanier's history and diversity. Application deadline: 26 June 2026, 11:59 pm Eastern Daylight Time, via the online form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
